commands.review_summary:
This PR changes `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` to build the code-mode worker URL test inputs and expectations with Node path/URL helpers instead of hardcoded POSIX `file:///repo` strings.

PR surface: Tests +8. Total +8 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence executed reproduction was performed in this read-only review. Source inspection shows the current test expectation is POSIX-specific, but the PR does not provide Windows or after-fix terminal proof.

target.title: test(code-mode): fix cross-platform worker URL test
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the `resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl` test to use `pathToFileURL()` from `node:url` instead of hardcoded `file:///repo/...` strings.

## Problem

The original test hardcoded POSIX-style file URLs:

```ts
testing.resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl("file:///repo/dist/agents/code-mode.js")
// expected: "/repo/dist/agents/code-mode.worker.js"
```

On Windows, `pathToFileURL()` produces `file:///C:/repo/...` — so the hardcoded path both in the input and the expected `.pathname` fails.

## Fix

Use `pathToFileURL(path.join(...))` for both the input URLs and the expected `workerPath`, so the test constructs file URLs the same way Node.js does on the current platform:

```ts
const workerPath = pathToFileURL(path.join(path.sep, "repo", "dist", "agents", "code-mode.worker.js")).pathname;
const stableUrl = pathToFileURL(path.join(path.sep, "repo", "dist", "agents", "code-mode.js")).href;
const hashedUrl = pathToFileURL(path.join(path.sep, "repo", "dist", "selection-abc123.js")).href;

expect(testing.resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl(stableUrl).pathname).toBe(workerPath);
expect(testing.resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl(hashedUrl).pathname).toBe(workerPath);
```

Test intent is unchanged — `resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl` must map both the stable `agents/code-mode.js` and any hashed `selection-*.js` to `agents/code-mode.worker.js`.

## Note on `disposeVmAfterRun`

PR #97451 also included a `disposeVmAfterRun()` wrapper to catch `interrupted` errors from `vm.dispose()`. After reviewing `quickjs-wasi@3.0.0` source (`dist/index.js` lines 1505–1521), `QuickJS.dispose()` only nulls JS references (`this.exports = null`, `this.instance = null`, etc.) and does **not** call back into the WASM runtime — so it cannot throw an `interrupted` error. ClawSweeper was correct to flag this. The wrapper has been removed from this PR.

The `interrupted` error in code-mode comes from `vm.evalCode()` / `drainPendingJobs()` during execution (via `throwIfException → JSException`), which is already caught by the `catch (error)` block in `runVmExecution` and handled via `isQuickJsInterruptedError` at lines 529 and 767.

## Changes

```
src/agents/code-mode.test.ts  — 1 file, cross-platform URL construction
```
